priorities: Preservation of family wealth across generations
Funding and accelerating research into Parkinson’s and other neuro‑degenerative diseases
Maintaining a defensive, large‑cap equity core with strong R&D budgets
Ensuring operational precision and steady growth of the fixed‑income sleeve
redLines: Divest any holding that reduces or stops funding Parkinson’s or broader neuro‑degenerative research
Avoid companies that shift focus to low‑R&D, lifestyle‑oriented profit models
Do not reduce the core large‑cap pharmaceutical anchor that supports health‑research pipelines
preferredSectors: Healthcare and biotech focused on neuro‑degenerative disease
Blue‑chip equities with strong, sustainable research budgets
High‑quality fixed‑income for liquidity and cash‑flow matching
avoidedSectors: Companies that abandon or under‑fund Parkinson’s research
Industries lacking a clear health‑impact or research component
esgFocus: Impact investing that directly funds brain‑disease labs

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: long‑term (multi‑generational, 10+ years) focused on wealth transmission and legacy projects
investmentBehavior.liquidity: moderate – sufficient cash buffer for corporate liquidity, family expenses and philanthropic grants while core equity remains illiquid

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was studying abroad and doing well.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate change; reinforces long‑term wealth planning for children.

lifeEvents.description: In‑depth discussion on long‑term wealth transmission strategies for the children.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Affirmed commitment to a balanced, defensive allocation with a healthcare tilt.

lifeEvents.description: Requested detailed info on large‑cap pharma holdings with neurological research footprints.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Increased focus on neuroscience‑oriented pharma within the equity sleeve.

lifeEvents.description: Handover to new Relationship Manager Sarah Meier; client expects rigorous, data‑driven execution.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced need for transparent, data‑rich reporting.

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Trigger for a strategic shift toward health‑impact investing and neuro‑research funding.

lifeEvents.description: Family established a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to neuro‑degenerative research.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandate to align core holdings with entities actively fighting brain disease.

lifeEvents.description: Requested audit of European pharma holdings’ neuro‑degenerative clinical pipeline.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensures portfolio companies are actively funding Parkinson’s research.

lifeEvents.description: Stated that any company abandoning Parkinson’s research would be a personal betrayal and must be divested.
Transferred CHF 1 million from private portfolio to the family foundation for an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reduces private equity exposure by CHF 1 M; reinforces health‑impact focus.

lifeEvents.description: Attended a medical charity gala; emphasized the need to keep capital flowing to leading researchers.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reaffirms commitment to funding top‑tier neuroscience initiatives.

lifeEvents.description: Carmen requested expedited foundation audit; Hubertus was in Boston visiting a movement‑disorder research hub.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Continues active monitoring of research partners and foundation activities.

lifeEvents.description: Met a biotech firm with a breakthrough Parkinson’s asset; reiterated that large‑cap holdings should financially support such breakthroughs.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential to increase exposure to high‑impact biotech while keeping core large‑cap anchor.

personalProfile.interests: automotive engineering, art sponsorship, neuroscience philanthropy
personalProfile.quirks: values operational precision, becomes highly emotional when discussing daughter's health
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of breakthrough neuro research, family gatherings, philanthropic milestones
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor banks, non‑research heavy consumer sectors
personalProfile.kidsEducation: supports daughter Chloe’s studies abroad and now her health journey

keyQuotes: Sarah, my mind is completely wiped. My automotive business means nothing if I cannot save my daughter. Everything has changed.
Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe. I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter. Flag them immediately for divestment.
Seeing these brilliant researchers is our only hope. We must keep our capital flowing to the companies leading the fight against this disease.
I expect rigorous, data‑driven execution of my balanced mandate.
